lifecycle_relationships.invariant: A classified artifact request is propagated into the active turn.
The propagated request contract is enforced before a tool call.
Request-scoped enforcement is cleared during finalization.
The canonical writer hands exact artifact bytes to the receipt verifier.
Verified artifact state survives until the turn finalizer selects delivery.
Normal gateway dispatch owns one artifact delivery path.
Streaming gateway dispatch owns the parallel artifact delivery path.
Normal gateway delivery reaches Telegram only through the verified descriptor.
Streaming delivery reaches Telegram only through the verified descriptor.
Verified Telegram descriptor outcomes transition the durable receipt.
Safe-mode decisions constrain plugin discovery for the session lifecycle.
Restricted plugin state is preserved through startup lifecycle preparation.
Fallback activation cannot exceed the enforced provider capability cap.
Fallback recovery restores primary runtime authority.
Persisted job allowlists remain bounded when scheduler toolsets are resolved.
Cron policy recovery updates the persisted restriction rather than expanding defaults.

sites.rationale: Canonical writer entrypoint that preserves the selected terminal backend before artifact verification.
Descriptor and parent-chain validation boundary for written artifact bytes.
Pins the task-owned parent while the terminal backend writes the registered artifact.
Binds a held delivery descriptor to the durable written receipt hash and byte count.
Persists the verified written byte identity before gateway delivery.
Durably records gateway and actual Telegram transport transitions.
Persists terminal failure before registry eviction and bounded cleanup.
Atomic fail-closed receipt persistence boundary.
Reconstructs only hash-matching task-owned artifacts for startup reconciliation.
Bounded startup and periodic receipt reconciliation only scans existing trusted roots under the receipt lock.
Gateway lifecycle invokes startup receipt recovery and owns the five-minute cancellable reconciliation task.
Cancellable five-minute loop prevents abandoned receipt cleanup from depending on a subsequent artifact request.
Identity-bounded cleanup boundary that never recursively deletes user content.
Clears request-local policy while preserving stale durable state on persistence failure.
Terminalizes descriptor validation failures before any Telegram provider attempt.
Non-streaming durable delivery completion boundary.
Non-streaming ambiguous delivery recovery boundary.
Streaming document delivery and safe failure-notice boundary.
Telegram descriptor open boundary linked to the durable receipt.
Counts each actual Telegram Bot API document transport call.

enforcement boundary for the safe mode contract.
Class container inherits the safe-mode receive-readiness boundary from its concrete liveness methods.
Publishes ready only when the polling updater and real receive evidence are both present.
Completed polling receives supply the evidence required to leave recovering readiness.
Inbound polling updates supply the evidence required to leave recovering readiness.
Scheduling recovery clears stale receive evidence before the retry task can report readiness.
Every polling start clears prior receive evidence until a new receive cycle completes.
Network recovery clears stale receive evidence before reconnect handling begins.

module-scope contract imports are reviewed as propagation dependencies.
module-scope guardrail dependencies are reviewed as enforcement dependencies.
module-scope delivery validation dependencies are covered by the base adapter contract.
module-scope cron schema dependencies are reviewed as job-policy decisions.
module-scope fallback guard dependencies are covered as enforcement.
module-scope fallback policy dependencies are covered as enforcement.
Propagates an explicit toolset selection into a batch agent without widening cron authority.
Class container inherits token matches from child CLI methods; it is not a cron execution boundary.
Initializes interactive CLI toolsets and does not construct scheduled cron agents.
Displays interactive CLI toolset state without changing cron authority.
Reports interactive CLI status only; it does not resolve cron toolsets.
Displays interactive tool configuration without executing a cron job.
Displays interactive toolset configuration without executing a cron job.
Displays configuration and does not apply scheduled-job authority.
Routes interactive commands; cron policy is enforced in the scheduler path.
Reloads MCP tools for the interactive CLI and cannot widen a cron run.
Module-level config defaults mention toolsets but do not resolve scheduled-job authority.
Rejects malformed cron enabled and disabled authority overlays before resolution.
Class container inherits child toolset matches; concrete scheduler resolution is registered separately.
Consumes the scheduler's pre-resolved enabled and disabled toolset envelope.
CLI entrypoint toolset selection is independent from scheduled cron execution.
Class container inherits fallback token matches from child status methods.
Reads fallback status for display and cannot activate or widen fallback authority.
Class container inherits child fallback matches; concrete transitions are registered separately.
Surfaces a pending fallback activation without changing provider authority.
Makes the agent-level fallback activation decision through the guarded helper.
Checks pending fallback state used to preserve the active authority boundary.
Fallback token is used only to shape reasoning metadata, not provider activation.
Safe mode bypasses both user and project behavioral configuration before CLI defaults are resolved.
The CLI entrypoint owns safe-mode startup and cleanup for the request lifecycle.
The class container inherits safe-mode matches from concrete startup and adapter-construction methods registered separately.
Safe mode permits only the bundled Telegram recovery adapter and rejects every other platform.
Gateway startup computes and records recovery-transport readiness for the active safe-mode lifecycle.
This readiness boundary reports safe mode ready only after the bundled Telegram recovery transport is connected and has current receive-path evidence.
Class container inherits artifact-only request-token discovery from its concrete fail-closed property and parser helpers.
Invalid overlays and artifact-only directives resolve to a restrictive artifact request boundary.
Module constants define the rescue overlay schema and path but do not start or retain a session lifecycle.
Malformed overlay input is converted to an artifact-only request restriction rather than permitting execution.
Validates recorded artifact-only request evidence but does not make or apply a live request decision.
Reads and validates the rescue overlay, rejecting absent or malformed state into the restrictive decision path.
Exposes the overlay restriction for callers that must deny artifact-only execution.
The parsed rescue overlay decides whether artifact-only requests remain permitted.
Applies the parsed rescue overlay restriction before callers allow artifact-only execution.
Class container restores artifact-only request evidence through concrete telemetry methods but does not own request authority.
Checks persisted artifact-only request evidence for corruption without making or enforcing the active request decision.
Aggregate class container retains artifact-only request evidence and has no request authority.
Validates telemetry event payload fields, including historical artifact-only request labels, without applying request authority.
Restores validated telemetry state containing artifact-only request evidence but does not decide or enforce current request authority.
